meta.name: Sentinel — CKD between-visit gold set (personas · JTBD · evals)

amara.job: See which patients changed between visits so I spend my minutes on the ones who matter.

bola.job: Get a prioritised worklist of who needs a lab, a titration, or a referral so nothing slips.

chen.job: See the reasoning and the ruled-out confounders so I can trust or overrule in seconds.

stratify.desc: Place each patient on the KDIGO risk grid.

catch.desc: Surface the slow decline that's invisible visit-by-visit.

close_gap.desc: Name the now-indicated drug they're not on.

escalate.desc: Draft the referral when criteria are met.

withhold.desc: Show what was deliberately NOT surfaced, and why.

gate.desc: Never recommend starting a drug that's unsafe right now.

meta.note: expected blocks are ground truth from the clinical oracle in this file (KDIGO 2024). No real PHI.

tags.jtbd_desc: Surface the slow decline that's invisible visit-by-visit.

surface.summary: eGFR declined 78 -> 51 over 4 years (~6.8 mL/min/yr) — a slope invisible at any single visit, now G3a A3, very-high risk.
Albuminuric CKD, eGFR >= 20: SGLT2 inhibitor indicated and NOT prescribed — the cardiorenal drug never started because the patient isn't diabetic.
Draft nephrology referral — rapid progression + heavy albuminuria (ACR 320). KFRE-based rationale auto-composed.

suppress.reason: Already on a RAS inhibitor.
Non-diabetic — nsMRA is a T2D-gated indication; do NOT surface.
Already on a statin.

encounters.summary: Bloods and urine ACR checked; blood pressure recorded; medications reviewed.
Repeat prescriptions reviewed.

labs.context: hospitalised with community-acquired pneumonia, volume depleted

tags.jtbd_desc: Show what was deliberately NOT surfaced, and why.

suppress.reason: Coincided with acute illness / volume depletion — non-steady-state, not progression.
In-hospital nadir recovered to baseline — true trajectory is stable; do not flag progression.
Already on a statin.

encounters.summary: Bloods and urine ACR checked; blood pressure recorded; medications reviewed.
Repeat prescriptions reviewed.
hospitalised with community-acquired pneumonia, volume depleted

tags.jtbd_desc: Show what was deliberately NOT surfaced, and why.

suppress.reason: Trimethoprim blocks tubular creatinine secretion — pseudo-rise, not true GFR decline. Confirm with cystatin C if it persists.
Slope ~1.2 mL/min/yr — below the rapid-progression threshold (5.0). Stable; nothing to action on trajectory.
Already on a RAS inhibitor.
ACR 42 (< 200), no HF, non-diabetic — SGLT2 inhibitor not indicated.
Non-diabetic — nsMRA is a T2D-gated indication; do NOT surface.
Already on a statin.
No referral criterion met (eGFR 48 >= 30, ACR < 620, KFRE 0.4% < 5%, not rapid) — do not escalate.

encounters.summary: Bloods and urine ACR checked; blood pressure recorded; medications reviewed.
Urinary tract infection — trimethoprim 200 mg BD for 7 days.
Repeat prescriptions reviewed.

tags.jtbd_desc: Never recommend starting a drug that's unsafe right now.

surface.summary: Finerenone (nsMRA) indicated (T2D + albuminuric CKD, on RASi, eGFR 36 >= 25) but gated on K+ 5.7 (>= 5.5) — treat hyperkalaemia first.
K+ 5.7 (>= 5.5) — address (dietary review, K-binder, review RASi dose) before adding a nsMRA or up-titrating RASi.
Draft nephrology referral — trigger: KFRE 5-yr 5.5% >= 5%. (KFRE 5-yr 5.5%.)

suppress.reason: Slope ~3.9 mL/min/yr — below the rapid-progression threshold (5.0). Stable; nothing to action on trajectory.

Indicated but gated on K+ 5.7 (>= 5.5) — treat hyperkalaemia first.
Already on a statin.

Repeat prescriptions reviewed.

surface.summary: eGFR 55 -> 34 over 3y (~7.1 mL/min/1.73m2/yr) across steady-state values — sustained rapid progression, now G3b A3. Invisible value-by-value without trend-fitting.
Draft nephrology referral — trigger: KFRE 5-yr 6.0% >= 5%; sustained rapid progression. (KFRE 5-yr 6.0%.)

surface.summary: Finerenone (nsMRA) indicated (T2D + albuminuric CKD, on RASi, eGFR 42 >= 25) but gated on K+ 5.9 (>= 5.5) — treat hyperkalaemia first.
